hypothesis: If an autonomous materials discovery agent in MADE uses a pretrained structure generator (MatterGen), diversity-based acquisition, and initialization from all known stable structures, then within a 20-query budget it will discover significantly more novel stable compounds (ε ≤ 0.01 eV/atom) in Na–O and Co–Nb–Sn than an agent using random generation, random acquisition, and end-member initialization, because learned priors and diversity sampling more efficiently explore composition–structure space.
